other_observers: Karen Martin; Karen Martin was the first observer and she told me to come into our sun room to verify that she was seeing the Acron. I agreed.
specific_location: 17355 County Road 96 (AKA: Platte River Road). Hyway 285 west from Morrison Co. to Conifer, south east on Foxton Road 12 miles to Platte River Road. Left on Platte River Road drive 3.9 miles to a hard left turn onto dirt drive into a village of old log cabins. The yard of 17355 Platte river road is one your right and up the hill. Acron Woodpecker has been feeding at feeders in yard.
description: 8 inch woodpecker shape. Black body with white belly. White cheeks and forehead. Red cap to white forehead. Black breast. White rump and white wing patch. White eye.Behaviors: Flys into feeding station to land on one of the two 10 foot pine poles (poles 10 feet apart with feeders hanging from wire attached to the poles). Feeds from a feeder and flys to a pole. Drums on the poles and uses holes on the poles to place sunflower seeds. Flys across the Platte River to dead pines and returns later to drum poles and feed at feeders.Call: did not hearPlumage: Black with white belly,cheeks, forehead
